title: Natural Gas Production (Gross, incl. Reinjected & Flared) - comparator countries (USGS)
notes: Comparator series from a SINGLE source that publishes the same table for every country (USGS Minerals Yearbook, Volume III Area Reports), so Iran and the comparators are measured identically rather than stitched from per-country national statistics. IRAN'S OWN USGS FIGURE IS INCLUDED as a distinct series alongside the comparators, and is NOT merged with or adjudicated against Iran's existing primary-sourced series on the same chart -- where the two disagree, both stand as separately-labelled series (a same-source cross-check is the point). Note the era gap: Iran's existing series on these charts is 1954-1980 (pre-revolution, USGS historical volumes); this comparator data is 2012-2023. It is the SAME measure from the SAME publisher, so it belongs on the same chart, but the chart will show two separated time blocks rather than one continuous run -- there is no USGS country-table data on disk for 1981-2011 and none was fabricated to bridge it. Values normalised to metric tons (the source's per-table default unit varies by country: Iran/Turkiye tables are in metric tons, Saudi/Iraq tables in thousand metric tons). USGS's own e=estimated / r=revised flags are carried per row. Where a later edition revised a value, the later edition wins. || status=new because GROSS gas production (including gas reinjected, flared and vented) is a genuinely different measure from the marketed/dry-basis series, and merging the two would overstate output for any country reporting on a gross basis. Only SAU and VEN publish it, so this is a thin chart -- flagged for the owner to decide whether it earns its place. || Coverage: SAU:2014-2021; VEN:2014-2022.

title: Motor Vehicle Production - comparator countries (OICA)
notes: Adds comparator lines to Iran's motor-vehicle chart from OICA's single multi-country survey table (the same survey for every country), so Iran and the comparators are measured identically. IRAN IS IN THE OICA TABLE ITSELF (OICA sources Iran's figure to CCFA), and that Iranian figure is included here as its own series -- it is NOT merged with, or adjudicated against, the chart's existing Iranian series (Wikipedia-sourced to 2005) or the CBI/SAPCO series proposed separately in enrichment_industry_energy.csv. Where they disagree, all stand as separately-labelled series. Coverage: 8 of the 11 roster countries -- IRN, TUR, RUS, USA, ARG, ESP, ITA, KOR -- for 2019, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024 (OICA's PDF publishes those five columns; 2020 is not among them). SAU, IRQ and VEN are absent because OICA does not survey them: they have no reportable vehicle-manufacturing industry. That is an absence in the source, not a retrieval gap. OICA's own scope qualifiers are preserved per row (Iran 'yearly only', Argentina 'cars and LCV only') -- they are NOT silently harmonised away, and a reader comparing Argentina to Iran should know Argentina's figure excludes heavy vehicles.
